docs.title: Welcome to Soma Curation Documentation
docs.text:
soma_curation is a package designed for bioinformaticians to facilitate soma data curation in single cell RNA sequencing projects. The documentation provides an in-depth reference to our API and functions.

There are also example notebooks on how to create and append to an atlas.

docs.title: Atlas
docs.text:
Bases: BaseModel

AtlasManager class is designed to manage the creation, deletion, and schema management for single-cell RNA sequencing atlases.

  • atlas_name: str The name of the atlas.
  • globals_: DatabaseSchema The schema defining the structure and validation rules for the data.
  • storage_directory: Path The directory where the atlas is stored.
  • context: soma.SOMATileDBContext The context for TileDB-SOMA operations, with a default factory function.

Methods: - exists: Checks if the atlas already exists. - create: Creates a new atlas. - delete: Deletes an existing atlas.

handler: python options: show_root_heading: true show_source: true

docs.title: experiment_path property
docs.text:
Compute the path to the experiment directory.

  • Path The path to the experiment directory.

docs.title: version property
docs.text:
Compute the path to the experiment directory.

  • Path The path to the experiment directory.

docs.title: create()
docs.text:
Create a new atlas.

This method initializes the experiment directory, sets up metadata, and creates necessary collections and dataframes.

docs.title: delete()
docs.text:
Delete an existing atlas.

This method removes the directory and all its contents.

docs.title: exists()
docs.text:
Check if the atlas already exists.

  • bool True if the atlas exists, False otherwise.

docs.title: Dataset
docs.text:
Bases: BaseModel

AnnDataset class is designed to validate and standardize AnnData objects according to a given database schema.

  • artifact: ad.AnnData The AnnData object containing the data matrix.
  • database_schema: DatabaseSchema The schema defining the structure and validation rules for the data.

Methods: - validate: Validates the AnnData object against the provided schema. - standardize: Standardizes the AnnData object by normalizing and computing required columns.

handler: python options: show_root_heading: true show_source: true

docs.title: Create an Atlas
docs.text:
In [ ]: Copied! 
!pip install data_curation
!pip install data_curation In [ ]: Copied! 
import soma_curation.sc_logging as lg
from soma_curation.schema import get_schema
from soma_curation.atlas.crud import AtlasManager
from soma_curation.dataset.anndataset import AnnDataset
from soma_curation.constants.constants import dummy_anndata

# Set the log level to info
lg.info()
import soma_curation.sc_logging as lg from soma_curation.schema import get_schema from soma_curation.atlas.crud import AtlasManager from soma_curation.dataset.anndataset import AnnDataset from soma_curation.constants.constants import dummy_anndata # Set the log level to info lg.info() In [ ]: Copied! 
db_schema = get_schema()
db_schema = get_schema() In [ ]: Copied! 
am = AtlasManager(atlas_name="human", globals_=db_schema, storage_directory="~/data_curation")
am.create()
# am.delete() # to delete
am = AtlasManager(atlas_name="human", globals_=db_schema, storage_directory="~/data_curation") am.create() # am.delete() # to delete In [ ]: Copied! 
# Assuming that you fetch a cool dataset from somewhere
anndata = dummy_anndata()
# Assuming that you fetch a cool dataset from somewhere anndata = dummy_anndata() In [ ]: Copied! 
# Create a Phenomic dataset using our schema, errors will pop up
# if this dataset does not follow the schema specified in
# db_schema.VALIDATION_SCHEMA
dataset = AnnDataset(artifact=anndata, database_schema=db_schema)
# Create a Phenomic dataset using our schema, errors will pop up # if this dataset does not follow the schema specified in # db_schema.VALIDATION_SCHEMA dataset = AnnDataset(artifact=anndata, database_schema=db_schema) In [ ]: Copied! 
# Standardize the columns
# This will run a general standardization pipeline, which involves:
# (a) Fixing the gene names
# (b) Embedding and predicting cell types
# (c) Normalizing the arrays
dataset.standardize()
# Standardize the columns # This will run a general standardization pipeline, which involves: # (a) Fixing the gene names # (b) Embedding and predicting cell types # (c) Normalizing the arrays dataset.standardize()

docs.title: Create an Atlas¶
docs.text: This notebook demonstrates how to create a Phenomic SOMA atlas with a predefined schema, create a Dataset that adheres to that schema, and append information to it.
